results.description:
DESCRIPTION:THE PURPOSE OF THE PROPOSED RESEARCH IS TO ENHANCE EXISTING EFFORTS TAKING PLACE IN WASHINGTON STATE TO IDENTIFY EVIDENCE-BASED SOLUTIONS UTILIZING PORTABLE AIR CLEANERS TO IMPROVE INDOOR AIR QUALITY IN SCHOOLS. ACTIVITIES: THE PROJECT WILL 1) IMPLEMENT A CLASSROOM-BASED INTERVENTION USING PORTABLE AIR CLEANERS TO REDUCE EXPOSURE TO WILDLAND FIRE SMOKE ACROSS 10 SCHOOLS IN WASHINGTON STATE; 2) OBSERVE THE IMPACT OF THE INTERVENTION USING PORTABLE AIR CLEANERS ON RESPIRATORY HEALTH OF STUDENTS USING A RANDOMIZED CONTROLLED STUDY DESIGN WITHIN SCHOOL; AND 3) ENGAGE WITH PARTICIPATING SCHOOLS TO ADAPT AN EXISTING HANDS-ON AIR QUALITY CURRICULUM AIMED AT INCREASING ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH LITERACY ON THE TOPIC OF AMBIENT SMOKE, AIR QUALITY AND HEALTH. SUBRECIPIENT:OUTCOMES:THE EXPECTED OUTPUTS INCLUDE: 1) A MODEL OF SIZE-FRACTIONED PARTICLE INFILTRATION IN BOTH RURAL AND URBAN SCHOOLS THAT TAKES INTO ACCOUNT BUILDING TYPE, VENTILATION PARAMETERS, AND EXISTING FILTRATION TYPE; 2) DIRECT EVALUATION OF THE EFFICACY OF REDUCING EXPOSURE TO WILDLAND FIRE SMOKE USING AN APPROPRIATELY SIZED PORTABLE AIR CLEANERS WITH AUTO MODE IN CLASSROOM SETTINGS; AND 3) MEASURES OF THE ASSOCIATION BETWEEN INDOOR AIR QUALITY AND RESPIRATORY HEALTH IN A DIVERSE COHORT OF ELEMENTARY SCHOOL CHILDREN. 

DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROGRAM INCLUDE THE SCIENTIFIC COMMUNITY, STATE AND LOCAL ENTITIES WORKING WITH SCHOOL DISTRICTS, AND ELEMENTARY SCHOOL CHILDREN AND THEIR FAMILIES. YAKAMA NATION AIR QUALITY SECTION WILL WORK WITH THE UNIVERSITY OF WASHINGTON RESEARCHERS TO RESPOND TO TRIBAL PROGRAM NEED, ENSURE RELEVANCE TO THE COMMUNITY, PARTICIPATE IN DEVELOPING CULTURALLY-AWARE EDUCATIONAL MATERIAL THAT IS IN-LINE WITH REQUIREMENTS AND REGULATIONS APPLICABLE ON YAKAMA NATION TRIBAL LAND, AND HELP ENSURE THAT THE RESEARCH TEAM DOES NOT INADVERTENTLY VIOLATE TRIBAL SOVEREIGNTY.
